element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players begin with settlers to establish first cities immediately
All map unexplored except immediate vicinity; exploration is stated as top early-game priority
Cities harvest food, trade, and production from nearby tiles with different terrain types and special resources
Cities may build units, city improvements, or wonders of the world
Players begin with primitive technology; research is stated as focal point; advances grouped by historical eras

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game (as part of initial unit variety)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Most units can attack; analogous to chess pieces with different functions
New concept in Civ II: diplomatic reputation affects relations and war declarations
Civilizations may declare war and conquer enemy cities; elimination occurs when all player cities conquered

element_introduction_order.element: Advanced space technology and spaceship construction
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: One of three victory conditions; requires advanced technology to reach Alpha Centauri

typical_relief_triggers: Completing city expansion to new territory
typical_tension_triggers: Competition for limited map resources and expansion space
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based with cyclical tension: early exploration phase (low tension), expansion competition (moderate tension building), diplomatic/military conflicts (high tension), technological race (persistent moderate tension)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Scenario presets and random map generation allow optional historical/fictional situations

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty: settlers establish cities, exploration of nearby terrain, basic unit management with primitive technology
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty: expansion competition with rival AIs, diplomatic reputation management, military encounters, technology acquisition decisions
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty: full-map territorial competition, complex warfare, diplomatic tensions, choice between military conquest or spaceship victory path
notable_spikes: First contact with rival civilizations (diplomatic/military decisions required)
Mid-game territorial saturation (competition for remaining land)
Advanced technology race (multiple civilizations pursuing spaceship victory)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early game focuses on settlers founding new cities until map settled; creates repeating cycle of exploration→settlement→resource gathering
Primitive technology limits early actions; scientific advances unlock new unit types, improvements, and strategic options; creates progression through historical eras
Players can pursue conquest, diplomatic/spaceship ascension, or time-based survival; each path emphasizes different game mechanics and creates different pacing
New to Civ II; player actions affect how rival AIs perceive and interact with player; creates meta-layer of long-term consequence management beyond immediate tactical gameplay
